mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, causing short-term equity sell-offs due to immediate earnings revision fears.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions relative to the 12-month trend do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in over 50% of periods where inflation exceeds 3.0% with policy rates below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and supports sustained consumption cycles, providing a reliable foundation for corporate earnings expansion and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth over any consecutive 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing and inventory misalignment, triggering rapid short-term market corrections as liquidity tightens.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding faster than the consumer price index signals improving productive capacity without inflationary pressure, allowing firms to raise margins and driving positive monthly equity returns during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat in over 60% of cases where industrial production growth outpaces inflation growth.
